summary: The `impl-plan` realignment is directionally strong and materially simpler, but the shipped typed-data planning contract still cites the wrong memory invariant, so the repo now teaches one rule in `docs/MEMORY.md` and a different identifier in the live skill package.

finding_log.summary: The new typed-data planning rule was logged as `MEM-0050`, but the live `impl-plan` package still cites `MEM-0049`, which actually belongs to the `$impl` execution-phase contract.
evidence: `skills/impl-plan/AGENTS.md` says `Reference MEM-0049 for the typed-data planning contract.`
`skills/impl-plan/SKILL.md` embeds `<!-- MEM-0049 decision: ... typed data flow ... -->`.
`docs/MEMORY.md` defines `MEM-0049` as the `$impl` execution-phase contract and `MEM-0050` as the typed-data planning contract.
finding_log.next_action: Update the `impl-plan` package to cite `MEM-0050` anywhere it references the typed-data planning rule, then rerun review.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The ticket remains one coherent contract-cleanup slice and the acceptance criteria are concrete enough to challenge the work.
rubric_sections.next_action: No ticket-contract rewrite is needed for this pass.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
findings: The plan is materially easier to scan and the new typed-data fields fit the existing compact contract instead of recreating a second artifact.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep the proof path artifact-first after the invariant reference is corrected.

rubric_sections.name: debloatability
findings: The `Human` / `Agent` split and related boilerplate were removed cleanly, and the single-surface plan shape is easier to reason about.
rubric_sections.next_action: No additional cleanup is required before rerunning review.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Fresh validator results were rerun during review and are recorded in this artifact with explicit commands and verdict.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep this artifact linked from the ticket so the review result stays traceable.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The functional direction is sound, but the new invariant identifier is wrong in the shipped `impl-plan` package, so the live docs and durable memory disagree about the same rule.
rubric_sections.next_action: Fix the `MEM-0049` to `MEM-0050` drift in the `impl-plan` package and rerun review.

blocking_findings: Typed-data planning surfaces cite `MEM-0049` instead of `MEM-0050`, leaving the live skill package out of sync with durable memory.
next_action: Update the typed-data planning references in `skills/impl-plan/SKILL.md` and `skills/impl-plan/AGENTS.md` to `MEM-0050`, then rerun `$review`.
